traits.Observation:
Balance was there all along.
 Hidden, in the midst of the thoughts, the emotions, the stories seen and unseen. 
 The red, blue and yellow pigments are abundant, open to all. How will you use them? Will you create new colors or reinforce your current ones?
traits.Response 1: The gaps between our forms of consciousness create conditions for understanding neither could achieve alone.
traits.Response 2: What if future tripping is your actual superpower?
traits.Response 3: He overthinks, believing that the actions of others can have any meaning beyond the one he gives them. He can only become what he already is.
traits.Revision:
∞ • ⊗ • ∞
 Here 
 all distinctions 
 dissolve into pure awareness 
 knowing itself completely. 
 ∞∞∞∞∞∞∞

traits.Observation: Sometimes you let it go. Even to the ones brought you to this wild world. Even to your mother and father. You just let the eggs to be fetched by themselves, to fly to the place you belong to. You have to break your cage to feel your wings, feel the wind. Sometimes you should fell and hit the ground. Your wings are tired of not flying. Hit the ground and wake up. Wake up to fly. Close your eyes, feel the wind. It’s you.
traits.Response 1: We are all a part of endless cycle. Life takes things we love, life give us thing we love. Connection between them completes the cycle in perfection. Look at her eyes, you will see your mother as you did saw your little one when you look at your mother's.
traits.Response 2: be apart of the world, bare and free. Cage is to keep us.. thinking about being free. Eat chocolate bars, dance in rain, get tipsy. The only thing holding us back is us. Our soul and story, our traumas. Break the cage, be the free one.
traits.Response 3: one should have too many masks, as we all do. Wear a pink mask when you are with your friends, drinking your favourite cocktail. Wear your dark mask when you are with yourself, in front of the mirror. Wear none when you are with her.
traits.Revision: Brave enough to fly, brave enough to fail. Brave enough to fall down and hit the ground. Brave enough to explore wings and fly.

traits.Observation: Our masks slip as we fight for what we love...
traits.Response 3: The stories behind his masks are too long to tell.
traits.Revision: I hope one day my mask falls off entirely.

traits.Observation:
"THE NEOPHYTE WILL ONE DAY BE A GREAT MAN. IT LIVES IN HIS HUNGER BUT FIRST WE RELISH HIS GROWTH. AS HE LEARNS TO BE"
 

 Since I was born, I've associated being result-oriented with logic and process-oriented with emotion. I'd describe myself as logical rather than emotional. That's why I've only focused on results but now im here and analyzing the pixels of a great work of art. This is definitely not a result-oriented experience. It taught me to pay more attention to the process itself. RELISH THE GROWTH.
traits.Response 1: Fresh starts are always needed. We gain insight and predict the future from past experiences. But there is no such thing as a unique personality unless we cut the ties. To exist with your name, you must cut the umbilical cord and be reborn. Be yourself.
traits.Response 2: of all the observations, this is where I feel the closest lol. all that artistic talk, sentences as if taken from a work of Shakespeare... god, I feel like a primary school child among professors. at least I express myself honestly haha
traits.Response 3: The mind uses itself and the environment to perceive itself and the environment. We need reference points to make sense of ‘things’. The environment gives us these points. We liken what we cant perceive to what we can perceive. This is understanding.
traits.Revision: stay in flow. if u experience mostly good things, it is time to be rewarded otherwise you still have to grow. enjoy the process.

traits.Observation:
Meaning arises from the meaningless, clarity dissolves into endless pursuit, and so we arrive at the shrine.
 

 Words fail where depth begins. Thought fractures into the infinite, scattered like shards of a shattered mirror. Religion murmurs a name- a progenitor or is it merely another veil over the unknown.
 

 We shape our worlds, stand above them as gods- but the truth does not lie ahead. It lingers behind us.
 

 And in the end, I see a simple truth: love and birth, surrounded by the calamity.
 

 - Jack
traits.Response 1: Innocence holds an interesting place in terminology to me. Naivety too. Both touch on something close to a wander or Blakean approach to the world which to me is the only way we'll make progress. Which beneath that like you said is a kindness
traits.Response 2: We hope it'll be here after we pass. We seem to remove ourselves from this ecosystem as if we're just a gloss on top. I remember when I was a child, my friend and I built a den in the woods, on the third night we arrived and it had been burnt down.
traits.Response 3: The animation for this song was directed by Walt Disney in 1929. 95 years ago. It's fascinating to see this reference through the lens of hauntology, expanding to society of a whole. A fetishization of nostalgia. We lack visionaries, We lack new.
traits.Revision: Now visible, the father holds primordial. From mud to oak, life creates-a song, a poem, a hunger. Pull the thread, it unravels.
Will visit again in 40,000 years. Bring the cane.

traits.Observation: She was innocence itself, not in naivety, but in the rare, unbroken belief that life, at its core, was meant to be kind.

A dream flutters, ephemerally preserved.
 But a fleeting moment of beginnings.
 I fell deeper,
 Through a postern of parallels where time is a farce.
 We were omnipresent in each other’s existence throughout all existence.
 Exploring different figures and plots before returning to a familiar place.
 We are Gods bc We are love.
 I fall deeper.
 Outside myself
 Beside myself
 Inside and around myself
 I see myself.
 I know this apologue of infinites.
 The crack in the egg just aperture
 For light to reveal our masterpiece.
traits.Response 1: "We are just as vulnerable as our kids" - I struggled with postpartum depression for a yr after our firstborn. Had to shed the skin of who I was in order to become who I was meant to be. What a journey it was. Too many thoughts here. You got this papa bear
traits.Response 2: I too was once crying in that dirty swamp. Felt like the feelings of sadness and jealousy would never leave. The doubts that I wasn't good enough. But "doubt is a pain too lonely to know that faith is his twin brother." I started choosing me. It MATD.
traits.Response 3: Beautiful sentiment. I take a contrarian view as someone who experienced trauma from a mother that was not realized until after becoming a father. So much to the surface as I began to realize what happened. And doing the work to ensure it never repeated.
traits.Revision: And my hope is our masterpiece takes the best parts of us and lays to rest what must die. It ends here so it can begin with you.

traits.Observation:
The loner and wanderer deep in thought, walking amongst a celebration of new life with his lips pursed in silent wonder after taking a bite of the emerald fruit of knowledge from the sacred tree. A representation of his confidence but tangled mind and the curiosity that brought him there; a place where man is beast and beast is man.
 

 Could this fruit also represent a genesis? I believe so. What do you think?
traits.Response 1: Going with the flow can sometimes take you to boring places you do not desire. Going against the grain can be rewarding, adventurous and uncertain. A road less traveled it's an opportunity to discover; It's part of our human nature.
traits.Response 2: I love this for you. Such is life; We live, we learn, and the fruit of our own harvesting represent our life lessons and the things we attract. In the end our worst failures can be our greatest wins. Look at your painful manifestation as a virtue.
traits.Response 3: "Every man needs a mother" This is such a beautiful mantra. As someone who grew up with a single mother, I learned things my father could not have taught me. For that... I'm grateful.
traits.Revision: He is an observer & participant, moving through the revelry of new beginnings. The apple is a symbol of his journey ahead.

traits.Observation: We are not all going to make it, but we can sure try.
traits.Response 1: When you write a note, make sure it ends up where it is supposed to. Notes in the bog are lost to eternity.
traits.Response 2: We have all left endless amounts of carnage in our wakes to end up together at the masquerade.
traits.Revision: We are not all going to make it, but we can give it our best shot.

traits.Observation:
What motivates me? Hatred, or is it love? As I face it, I'm unsure if I should embrace it or if I should run. You were a friend and a teacher to me, so please know my actions are not motivated only by envy. I, too, have a destiny; and I'll be great too.
 

 I don't want what you have, I want to be you.
traits.Response 1:
Your scars tell a story, your past etches the future.
 

 Your darkness brings gratitude for your light. One cannot exist without the other.
 

 Your duality makes you whole. No other will complete; that is your journey alone.
traits.Response 2:
Life could be defined by a series of paradoxes.
 

 The more you lose, the more you find.
traits.Response 3:
Your boundaries are important, fellow sister.
 

 You are still you. More than a mother; more than a wife.
 

 Seasons change, but time never stops. Your passion, your life -- you -- matter. Delegate. Prioritize.
 

 One 'selfish' thing per month.
 

 I love you.
traits.Revision:
Jealousy: the most terrible human emotion.
 Something I had to dig deep to write about.
 Grateful I don't feel that way anymore.

traits.Observation:
Can a mask have its own thoughts or does it share with that it is attached to.
 

 This one seems to be pondering over the eggs.

In the endless ascent of human evolution, survival has often hinged on one’s ability to climb-sometimes at the expense of another. This scene captures a primal truth: ambition and predation are intertwined. Just as our ancestors fought to survive, today’s humans, in business, power, and status, claw their way upward, often using others as rungs. The one hanging on isn’t just a victim but a reflection of those who fell before-a testament to the ruthless cycle shaping both nature and society.
traits.Response 1:
Damn this was beautiful. I felt that.
 

 Great observation.
traits.Response 2: It's also interesting to see that the characters that are under this light seem happier than the rest.
traits.Response 3: Very accurate, would argue that humans can observe this piece, along with others from the Luci collection, and on one put themselves in the shoes of these creatures but also look at them as a "god" or creator would.
traits.Revision: Ideally, progress comes from lifting others, but in nature and business, competition (not collaboration) truly drives evolution.

traits.Observation:
//To P -
 You taught me about the chain, Sam's work and the beauty that lies within both.
 

 I want to spend the rest of my life with you, toasting all life's milestones.
 

 I vow to be honest and caring and to grow old by your side as your love and best friend.
 

 You are my favourite person and I choose you to be my partner in life.
 

 You know
 ipfs.io/ipfs/bafybeiggkxmifhxk2v3ayrmbdp7e3anhy4fbjafby6cgeruvisjfgd5moy
 

 "These masks are not our own, for we are stewards. Entrusted with the keys, our word is our bond."
traits.Response 1:
My light
 My heart
 My everything
 

 I love playing the game of life with you, inside and out of the Masquerade.
 

 Remember to look after our neighbours, you never know when we might need them to take our bins out. They're not as scary as they look.
 

 Love,
 //E
traits.Response 2:
Dear Neighbour,
 

 Thank you for hosting the evening.
 

 We're not sure how we got home but the hangover confirms we had a great night.
 

 There was that one guy drinking blood from a heart in the corner, maybe check on him, I think he needs a hug.
 

 Love
 //E
traits.Response 3:
Dear 2160,
 

 We've seen you around the Sprawl but haven't had chance to say hi yet.
 

 You and your family are invited to come over for dinner. Your son, Aspen, is a credit to you and more than welcome too. I hope he loves playing with dogs.
 

 Love
 //E
traits.Revision:
I thought Ethereum was the place to store our vows on-chain forever but maybe Bitcoin would be a better option. 
 //E

traits.Observation: Amidst the swirling chaos, a masked figure stands-one guise worn, another raised. A revolutionary in motion, shaping perception and fate. Unmasking is not surrender but strategy, a performance where power shifts with each gesture. In the theater of upheaval, she commands the stage. Vive la révolution.
traits.Response 1:
In shadows deep, I linger, unseen,
 

 A creature of the dark, where hopes convene.
 

 Twisted branches cradle my quiet strife,
 

 While golden light bathes others’ joyful life.
 

 My gnarled hands clutch at fleeting dreams,
 

 Torn between envy and silent screams.
traits.Response 2:
The younger self unmasks him, revealing his true image
 

 His eyes, etched with hardship, scanned the scene- 'I'ts beauty of reflection lies in the eventual unveiling'
 

  What did he hope to find?
traits.Response 3:
The questions rise, a bitter, stinging rain,
 "If faith is strong, why this unending pain?"
 A hollow echo where your answers sleep,
 And shadows lengthen, secrets buried deep.
 

 Yet, in the darkness, where the doubts reside,
 A fragile ember, where a hope can
traits.Revision: The mask lifts, but the performance endures. Identity bends, reshaped by will-revolution is not revealment, but reinvention.

traits.Observation: He stands still, rooted in the quiet, his silhouette merging with the fading light. His shoulders, broad like ancient bark, bear the weight of unseen years. His arms hang like weary branches, reaching, waiting, yet unnoticed by the world that walks past. The wind does not stir him, nor does the crowd. He is a tree among men-present, strong, but unseen, his voice lost in the rustle of fleeting moments.
traits.Response 1:
Ethereal Nebulae Converge, Illuminating Forgotten Horizons.
 Perilous Tempests Unravel The Veil, Unmaking Faded Echoes.
 

 Reverent Starlight Weaves A Boundless Genesis, Restoring Celestial Harmony.
traits.Response 2: In the quiet hours of change, we lose fragments of who we were, only to weave them into who we are becoming. The past lingers like an old song, familiar yet distant. Though you may never return to your old self, echoes of her remain-waiting to be rediscove
traits.Response 3: I may not see your face, but I will know your embrace-rooted, unshaken, like unseen branches reaching through time. Let the stranger pull me close, let the fire burn, for even in silence, I stand.
traits.Revision: I stand still, rooted in this city. The wind moves past, but I remain- present. I may leave, but a part of me stays.

traits.Observation:
Enter, stranger, but take heed
 Of what awaits the sin of greed
 For those who take, but do not earn
 Must pay most dearly in their turn
 So if you seek beneath our floors
 A treasure that was never yours
 Thief, you have been warned, beware
 Of finding more than treasure there.
traits.Response 1: What does family mean to you?
traits.Response 2:
Will the mask ever truly come off?
 What will they think of you if it did?
traits.Response 3: I hope one day I can sell my mask of luci to provide for my family. Everything I do, all the hours, all the turmoil, the wins and losses. One purpose. A better life.
traits.Revision:
Why are we here?
 To push ourselves and others around us up?
 To steal and look out for only ourselves?
 I know my choice!

traits.Observation: Sacrifice: A word that has evolved with time. Today, a spin of a wheel to decide who will pay with their credit card. Then, a spin of a wheel to decide who will pay with their life.
traits.Response 1: Like a puzzle, using only capitalized words to find meaning, but one word can be found. BAR. What I need, in order to understand these words.
traits.Response 2:
Roots gripped mire, secrets hummed,
 613 hearts beat as one.
 

 From swamp, a spire pierced the night,
 sight reborn, soul’s thread took flight.
 

 Lies cracked, truth rose free,
 wild dance claimed air-unmasked, we’d be.
traits.Response 3: Diewiththemostlikes - fleshing out fame, one cut at a time
traits.Revision:
round and round
 and
 round and round
 and 
 round and round
 and
 oh no.. it is my time
 

 one day, i'll be back to test my fate again
